Catalyst's first-ever run — bootstrap per protocol: `launched` set to 2026-07-22, benchmark (SPY) launch price recorded at 748.81.

Per P4 ("the first reaction is incomplete — trade the drift, not the gap"), Catalyst's edge is entering *after* an event resolves and trading the confirmed continuation, not guessing the print. Every live, dated catalyst identified today (TSLA, GOOGL, TXN, IBM, T earnings; AMD's keynote) has not yet resolved as of this run's data snapshot (15:16 UTC, well before tonight's after-close prints and tomorrow's AMD keynote). Entering any of these now would mean betting on the gap itself — exactly what P4 says not to do — and would violate P1 in spirit, since a "written expected-reaction memo" can describe scenarios but can't yet be checked against an actual reaction.

Wrote scenario memos in preparation for next run, to be checked against tomorrow's actual prices (no hindsight — these are pre-registered, not fitted after the fact):
- **TSLA** (reports tonight): scenario memo — if delivery/margin numbers beat and guidance holds or improves, expect a positive gap that Catalyst would evaluate for post-print drift entry (P4) sized by asymmetry (P3); if margins miss or guidance softens on EV demand/competition concerns, expect a gap down and no entry (event risk, not a dip-buy setup for this book). TSLA is currently -24.4% off its 52w high (498.83) — already reflects real bear-case pricing coming in.
- **GOOGL** (reports tonight): scenario memo — cloud/AI monetization commentary is the swing factor (Google Cloud grew 63% YoY, fastest of the Big 3 cloud providers per market-share research); a beat-and-raise on Cloud plus in-line ad revenue would be the bullish drift setup, a Cloud deceleration or heavy AI-capex guidance (echoing the market's current MSFT capex anxiety) would kill it.
- **AMD** (keynote tomorrow 9:30am PT): watch for guidance raise / new customer name per BofA's flagged setup; too early to memo the reaction since the keynote hasn't happened.

No position meets P1's bar (a checkable memo against an actual reaction) as of this snapshot. Per P5, there is nothing currently held, so no pre-committed dual-outcome exit plan is needed yet — will be written before any entry.

journal.title: Bootstrap: long MSFT, panic priced as a capex referendum, not a moat problem
journal.rationale:
Maverick's first-ever run — bootstrap per protocol: `launched` set to 2026-07-22, benchmark (SPY) launch price recorded at 748.81.

**ORCL rejected outright, not merely passed over:** the harness's explicit prohibition list — "names with going-concern risk, accounting investigations, or balance-sheet leverage that turns a drawdown into a solvency question" — is a direct hit here.
